pattern: Every cell's next state depends only on its current eight neighbours, so encoding old and new state in two bits of each integer lets the whole board advance simultaneously in place before a final pass strips the old bit.
hints: All cells update from the same snapshot, so you must not let an already-updated neighbour influence a later cell.
Instead of a second board, pack the next state into an unused bit of each cell so the current state (bit 0) is still readable while you write the next state (bit 1).
First pass: count live neighbours from bit 0, apply the birth/survival rules, and OR in bit 1 when the next state is alive; second pass: shift every cell right by one bit to reveal the new board.

edge_inputs.description: single live cell dies from underpopulation
a blinker oscillates between horizontal and vertical
all live cells, corners survive but centers overpopulate

stress.description: random boards up to the maximum dimensions with roughly even life density
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randrange(1, max(1, min(size, 25)) + 1)
    n = rng.randrange(1, max(1, min(size, 25)) + 1)
    return [[[rng.randrange(0, 2) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]]
